body:
## Goal
Implement the requested change from the agent mention.

## Original request
> I think this is worth adding, but I would keep the first version conservative: `agent-self-approve` should be a final, opt-in PR approval gate, not an unbounded “merge whatever the agent made” mode.
> 
> Current fit with the codebase:
> 
> - Routes are centrally enumerated in [`triage.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/triage.ts#L12-L31), and explicit slash routes are resolved deterministically before model triage.
> - Prompt lookup is route-based in [`run.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/run.ts#L113-L126), so adding `agent-self-approve` needs a matching prompt.
> - Orchestrator handoffs are currently limited to `implement`, `review`, and `fix-pr` in [`handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L3-L7), with policy edges like `implement -> review`, `review -> fix-pr`, `fix-pr -> review` at [`handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L410-L466).
> - Initial orchestrator authorization also hardcodes the delegated route set in [`orchestrator-capabilities.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/orchestrator-capabilities.ts#L8-L12).
> - The existing human approval flow only dispatches `agent-implement.yml` from [`agent-approve.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-approve.yml#L71-L96), so `/approve` integration needs a small but explicit design choice.
> 
> Implementation approach I’d recommend:
> 
> 1. Add `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE`, default false everywhere. When false, the route should be unavailable to router/orchestrator and should never submit PR approval.
> 2. Add a new PR-only workflow, probably `.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml`, with `pull-requests: write`, memory/rubrics read-only, and a new `.github/prompts/agent-self-approve.md`.
> 3. Make the prompt high-level: product fit, repository goals, long-term maintainability, trust boundaries, automation risk, rubric alignment, and whether approving is actually the right next step. It should not duplicate the low-level review prompt.
> 4. Require structured output, e.g. `APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, or `BLOCKED`, plus rationale and follow-up context.
> 5. Submit the GitHub PR approval only from deterministic code after validating all of these:
>    - `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE == true`
>    - target is a PR
>    - agent verdict is exactly `APPROVE`
>    - PR head SHA has not changed since the agent inspected it
>    - normal review/rubrics signals are acceptable, or the self-approve prompt explicitly justifies overriding advisory rubrics
> 6. Extend orchestrator policy so `review -> agent-self-approve` is allowed when review synthesis says `SHIP` and self-approve is enabled. If self-approve returns changes needed, hand back to orchestrator with context so it can choose `fix-pr`.
> 7. For `@agent /approve xxx`, I would not let self-approve replace the initial human approval gate for starting work. I would instead have approved implementation chains opt into the self-approve final gate once a PR exists. That preserves the current meaning of `/approve` while enabling the “YOLO” end-to-end chain.
> 
> Execution plan:
> 
> 1. Update route types, explicit route parsing, access policy behavior, and orchestrator delegated route capability checks.
> 2. Add the self-approve prompt and workflow.
> 3. Add a typed CLI helper to parse self-approve verdicts and submit the PR approval against a checked head SHA.
> 4. Extend handoff policy/tests for `review -> agent-self-approve -> fix-pr/stop`.
> 5. Wire `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE` through router, approval, orchestrator, and docs.
> 6. Update architecture/config docs and add tests for default-disabled behavior, PR-only enforcement, stale-head rejection, and orchestrator routing.
> 
> One clarification before coding: should `agent-self-approve` be user-invokable as `@sepo-agent /agent-self-approve`, or should it be internal/orchestrator-only for the first version? My default would be internal/orchestrator-only unless explicitly enabled by `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE=true`.

@sepo-agent /implement

## Acceptance criteria
- Implement the requested change.
- Preserve existing behavior unless the request requires a change.
- Update tests or validation as needed.

---

Requested via discussion_comment at https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/153

comments.body:
**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-165/codex-25508067922`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/166

Implemented an opt-in `agent-self-approve` PR approval gate gated by `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE=false` by default, including a new workflow, prompt, typed self-approval CLIs, orchestrator handoff policy updates, authorization checks, docs, and focused regression coverage. Verified with `npm run build`, targeted node tests, YAML parsing for the touched workflows, and `git diff --check`.

title: Implement requested change
